title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Automation and Data Clien… · Behavior Case: Dns Rebinding Toctou
summary: A server-side preview fetcher accepts source URLs and must defeat SSRF, DNS rebinding, redirect bypass, hostile response data, and unsafe logging. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py dns rebinding toctou increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
